EncyDesc_MutantDiver1:
Mental health warning: Alterra provides complimentary therapy sessions following disturbing or stressful missions.

This creature appears to be native to this planet, originating <color=#55555588>from the corpses of the Aurora’s survivors</color>. As with many local lifeforms, this specimen shows high levels of aggression towards both native fauna and humans.

Please remain cautious when in proximity of local infected fauna, as there is a high chance that you are next. Do not look too closely; there is a non-zero chance that you may be observing the rotten, mutated, infested corpse of a friend or loved one.

Alterra requests you to avoid the specimen at all costs, look the other way, and remember the binding terms of your contract.

EncyDesc_Drifter:
A generally passive amalgamation of flesh that has been tasked with the duty of spreading the Red Plague. It does not appear to have any will of its own, and instead seems to be controlled by an unknown entity. Scans indicate a link between creatures infected with the Red Plague. Further investigation is recommended.

1. Amalgamation:
The creature appears to be formed from the flesh of many other species, scans indicate the presence of human tissue within this creature.

2. Destructive purpose:
This creature appears to exist for the sole purpose of spreading the Red Plague. Spore-like emissions of concentrated plague matter emit from this creature wherever it goes.

Assessment: Extreme caution is advised. Do not approach or interact with under any circumstances.
EncyDesc_MimicPeeper:
This peeper variant appears to have been infected by the plague bacterium, changing both its appearance and behavioural patterns. Enough of its body has been replaced by plagued flesh to designate it as a separate species.

1. Hind facing jaws:
The tail fin of this peeper variant has mutated into a set of sharp jaws, designed to easily feast on smaller fauna.

2. Unusual swimming pattern:
This creature uses the heft, and momentum of it's forebody to glide through the water at an alarming pace, often appearing to swim backwards when observed.

3. Unusual behaviour:
This peeper variant is hostile to all it encounters unlike its passive cousins.

Consumption of the flesh of this creature is not recommended and may lead to adverse and lethal side effects.

Observing this creature may reveal information surrounding the plague bacterium, designated "The Red Plague". Further study highly recommended.
EncyDesc_MimicOculus:
This specimen shows resemblance to the species designated 'oculus' but is biologically different enough to be designated as an entirely separate species.

Its internal structure lacks proper organs and is instead entirely composed of plagued matter. This impostor is also significantly larger than the original species. While visually similar to the Oculus, it appears to be merely pretending and invading this ecosystem, serving no purpose other than to replace the original species.

Assessment: Invasive species, do not eat
EncyDesc_PlagueBladderFish:
This mimicked bladderfish bears some resemblance to other species found on 4546B though has some notable differences.

- The body of this specimen has almost entirely been consumed by pustulous flesh. Any organs that it once had have been amalgamated into its body.
- Spiky protrusions cover this creature's body, primarily used to tear open the flesh of predators and prey alike.
- The creature is incredibly bloated, limiting its mobility. This does not seem to deter the creature however, and it is surprisingly hostile to all that it encounters.
It is unclear where this creature originated from, or its purpose. Further study recommended.
Assessment: Further study required; caution is advised
EncyDesc_InvertedSpadefish:
This specimen is actually a heavily mutilated spadefish. Through an elaborate process, plague matter has made several cuts into its ‘skin,’ splicing it into loose small portions that are then able to be flipped inward, resulting in its skin being turned “inside out” gradually. This process appears to be extremely painful.

Unique swimming pattern: The inversion of the skin of the spadefish has also affected its sensory organs, and by extension its equilibrium. The inverted spadefish can then be observed swimming upside down, with its mouth facing upwards. This disrupts its normal feeding instincts, causing it to slowly starve, leading to erratic and desperate behavior.

Assessment: avoid or incapacitate to put it out of its misery
EncyDesc_MutantBoomerang:
This creature is a heavily plague-infected corpse of a typical boomerang. Standard diagnoses suggest the creature is dead, although it is fully capable of movement, food consumption, responding to stimuli, and possibly reproduction.

1. Diet:
The mutant boomerang is ravenous and can be observed decimating local plant populations, also preying on fauna much larger than itself. The creature appears to utilize energy from its digested food for nurturing new plague cells.

2. Eyes:
The addition of multiple new, deep red eyes appears to be unintentional and the result of complex DNA conversion that occurs as a part of the assimilation process.

3. Teeth:
The boomerang’s serrated teeth which were originally suited for eating tough coral and vegetation are well suited for tearing through flesh.

Assessment: An undesirable and invasive disruption to the local ecosystem
EncyDesc_FleshStalker:
This specimen appears to be under the influence of the Red Plague bacterium. This particular specimen is exhibiting behavioural and appearance changes.
This creature seems to seems to have lost the will to take care of its health, and is no longer observed to sharpen its teeth on scrap metal.

The creature is notably more aggressive to all other living beings, including its own species.
Assessment: increased aggression renders this creature a threat to personnel and submersibles - avoid
EncyDesc_InfestedReefback:
This Leviathan class organism has fallen prey to the effects of the Red Plague bacterium. This creature now bears little resemblance to its original form and has been designated "Blisterback." The disease seems to have mutated faster than anticipated, granting it a variety of new abilities.

- The bacterium is now capable of mimicking more complex organic structures, manifesting in this case in the replacement of the Reefback's shell and eyes. These vital organs for survival and function have been replaced with a hard keratin structure; seemingly made up of matter harvested from elsewhere. This "blisterback" is no longer alive, nor can it see.
- Studying this creature may reveal other abilities of the plague bacterium
Assessment: Valuable research prospect. Passive, but infected - approach and treat with caution.
EncyDesc_MimicGasopod:
While visually similar to the native species designated ‘gasopod,’ this specimen was heavily infested by parasitic lifeforms. Its internal organs were fully replaced by plague matter, with only the skin and general form remaining intact.

1. Behavior:
The mimic gasopod seems to wander aimlessly, occasionally hunting larger lifeforms for food. This creature has a unique ability to use the dense muscles in its spine and tail to slam into prey, which can deal significant harm to its prey.

2. Infection spreading:
Instead of using the defensive mechanism seen in the normal gasopod, the mimic gasopod occasionally emits pods with high levels of cultivated Red Plague cells, often infecting any nearby fish.

Assessment: slow and clumsy, but fatal to inexperienced divers
EncyDesc_Observer:
A spiny glob of flesh with a large primary eye surrounded by teeth and multiple eyes on its sides. It exhibits unique traits compared to other plagued organisms, particularly showing a completely passive temperament and fleeing when approached.

1. "Meowing" Behavior:
Plague Cats will produce a high-pitched whine when observing something of interest, possibly to attract more Plague Cats to strengthen the group. Interestingly, this whine is either inaudible to or ignored by most other plague creations, as it does not influence nearby aggression.

2. Intimidating Teeth:
Likely intended as a sort of visual deterrent, the Plague Cat's teeth are actually harmless and serve only to intimidate possible predators and protect the main eyeball.

3. Stalking Behavior:
Plague Cats attempt to stay just out of reach of their current interest, generally swimming away when it gets too close. The vast majority of their nervous system is composed of ocular tissues, meaning they have trouble actually comprehending what they see. This occasionally causes them to ram eye-first into their target of interest, then bouncing off and swimming away.

Assessment: Physically harmless with an unclear connection to the hivemind - more research is required
Tooltip_InfectionPod: A pod of concentrated Red Plague matter. Use caution when handling.
EncyDesc_TeethTeeth:
This unique specimen, resulting from infectious damage to a member of the species designated ‘eyeye’, has experienced extensive alterations to its form and diet.

1. Mouth:
The teethteeth’s eyeball has deteriorated and been reduced to a gaping hole with tooth-like bony protrusions. While it has no proper throat, its stomach has been exposed to the water, allowing the creature to digest anything that enters its mouth, bypassing its mouth and esophagus. New muscular formations mean this creature can bite, with bites being potentially lethal.

2. Diet:
The teethteeth is able to digest almost anything organic due to its morphological changes. Therefore, its behavior has developed to include primitive predatory habits, though lacks proper hunting instincts.

Assessment: don’t let it bite you
EncyDesc_EyeyeCaptain:
This specimen appears similar to the native lifeform designated 'eyeye', but has multiple distinct features resulting from prolonged exposure to the Red Plague.

1. Eye:
Its eye can be detached due to muscle fibers that can rip then rapidly regrow and rebind to nearby muscle cells. Its eye can be launched like a projectile at nearby terrain, creatures, or humans to interact with the environment and harm its prey.

The eyeye captain has a larger body than the eyeye; this allows it to handle the recoil of the eye detaching from its socket to manipulate objects or creatures.

Assessment: Hostile - be wary of your surroundings. Ahoy, matey!
EncyDesc_PlagueFloater:
A large jellyfish-like creature that has been assimilated into the plague. This creature glides gracefully through its environment through currents, mercilessly and indiscriminately grabbing and consuming any prey that comes too close.

1. Tendrils:
This specimen features ten sharp tendrils that are used to incapacitate its prey, capable of quickly manipulating prey and transporting it towards its mouth. When not hunting, these tendrils float loosely in the water, secreting microscopic particles (now including plague cells) directly from its digestion system back and into the ecosystem.

2. Role in the ecosystem:
The scyphomedusoid can be observed hunting and consuming local fauna, including other 'plague creations.' This cycle of the plague hunting its own kind appears to be intentional; it promotes genetic diversity, keeps populations in check, helps disperse nutrients, and further establishes a self-sufficient ecosystem.

3. Artificial natural selection theory:
This creature generally only consumes and destroys oblivious prey, resulting in the culling of 'biologically inadequate' plague creations. Alongside its role of population control, these creatures may also serve to reinforce the existence of only the most successful and intelligent plague specimens.

Assessment: Extremely dangerous keystones of advanced plague ecosystems
EncyDesc_Hippopenomenon:
This specimen resembles a mutated turtle-like creature. Unusually, it does not appear similar to any other known species on the planet.

The creature exhibits unique inquisitive and social behavior, with a telepathic-like ability to beckon other species to particular locations.

Avoid skin-to-skin contact with this specimen; it is unknown if it is able to spread its infection when interacting. However, it appears to be relatively healthy and unharmed aside from its unusual behavior.

Assessment: investigate and follow, but stay cautious. Would like to be pet, but for you to do so is a liability
EncyDesc_Gilbert:
This specimen appears to harbor similarities with the native lifeform designated 'biter', but lacks the predator’s distinct aggressive behavior.
It exhibits a curiously symbiotic relationship with its creator after manifesting from the Plague Altar, and shows social behaviors not dissimilar to those seen in domesticated animal companions. Neural scans indicate a protective quality directed in favor of its creator.
Note: It is recommended that this creature’s DNA be analyzed for mutations of the Red Plague which may have caused this irregular social behavior.
Assessment: Reciprocate social interactions to maximize the specimen’s protective behaviors - You have a friend
EncyDesc_PhantomLeviathan:
This specimen is a relatively young, corrupted variant of the ‘ghost leviathan’. Deformities of internal organs and bone degradation suggest severe malnutrition.

1. Eye:
The phantom leviathan’s sensory organs have been reduced to one free-floating eye. Despite the lack of visible nerves and muscles to hold it in place, the creature behaves as if it has a full range of vision— possibly a full 360° view without limitations.

2. Poison:
The phantom leviathan constantly secretes a colorful unidentifiable compound. This ‘poison’ could be some sort of corrosive element, but may be a unique strand of virus (not the Red Plague). Regardless, it is highly dangerous and is used to disable the nervous systems of its prey.

3. Location:
This leviathan specimen has recently arrived to this location, likely incentivized by the imbalanced ecosystem that lacks any larger predators, therefore establishing it as a core component of the food chain.

Assessment: avoid at all costs
Ency_ChaosLeviathan: The Manifestation of the Hivemind: 'Chaos Leviathan'
EncyDesc_ChaosLeviathan:
Leviathan class species are vast organisms at the top of their respective food chains. This leviathan, however, is unique in that it exists as the manifestation of the Red Plague's hivemind, intricately designed and perfected by itself over millennia while in containment.

1. Ocular multiplicity:
Numerous independently rotating eyes allow for near-total environmental awareness. This adaptation supports highly effective prey detection across a wide radius, even in low-light or obscured conditions.

2. Quadrisected mandible structure:
The creature's jaw anatomy is split into four articulated segments. This allows the creature to extend its gape far beyond that of typical predators, enabling secure capture and immobilization of large prey.

3. Cephalopodal Posterior Structure:
The posterior anatomy of the Chaos Leviathan closely resembles that of the species designated 'Sea Dragon,' featuring eight powerful, tentacle-like appendages extending from end of the spine.

These limbs function as the creature’s primary method of propulsion and maneuverability, allowing for coordinated, multi-directional movement in a variety of environments and rapid bursts of speed.

4. Effects in proximity:
Infection readings near the creature display dangerous, nearly fatal levels of the Red Plague. This appears to induce severe hallucinations. The multitude of eyes alongside the creature's body combined with hallucinatory symptoms evokes the feeling of being perpetually watched from all directions.

5. Formation:
The avatar of the Red Plague's hivemind does not have a typical biological structure designed for aquatic environments. In fact, it appears to be compatible with countless types of environments regardless of their atmospheres and is capable of hibernating for millennia, which explains how it has thrived since its initial creation.

While in hibernation, the Red Plague absorbs low-energy particles and rays (particularly solar radiation) while gradually metamorphosing its own physiological structures and optimizing its genetic code. Essentially, the modern Red Plague is the result of a self-perpetuating algorithm that seeks to perfect itself. Built from the efforts of complex neural pathways and systems of specialized cells over thousands of years, the Red Plague has deemed this to be its ideal form.

Motivational note: Congratulations on getting this close and not dying from the infection! 

Assessment: Extreme threat level - Avoid in all circumstances
EncyDesc_FleshPlant1:
DNA: This flesh plant’s DNA is original rather than a mutated version, implying the plague has surpassed simple “infestation” and is instead capable of creating complex life forms.

1. Relationship:
The flesh plant has a symbiotic relationship with the plant informally designated “stabby,” allowing both to consume a larger amount of prey than what they could normally obtain on their own. This plant secrets a compound into the water that attracts fish to its location, while the stabby incapacitates prey for mutual digestion. It is unknown how they communicate.

2. Digestion:
The flesh plant has a unique property that allows it to consume creatures and then act as a host for new plague matter growth. The digested matter accumulates in the center of the plant then grows over time into plague matter. In the partially digested stage, this matter contains various resources that could be used for the development of advanced plague technology.

EncyDesc_Infection_REPLACE: У этого существа наблюдаются признаки тяжелой и агрессивной бактериальной инфекции. - Красные, протекающие пустулы образуют взаимосвязанные сети вокруг очагов инфекции. - Поведение субъекта выглядит гораздо более агрессивным, чем обычно, что указывает на психологические симптомы. - Исследование корпорации Альтерра показало, что бактерия способна мутировать и изменять генетический код инфицированного человека. - Спектроскопическое сканирование показало, что плоть существа намного горячее, чем обычно, почти на точке плавления. - Бактерия не похожа ни на что, с чем когда-либо сталкивалось или что было зарегистрировано в истории человечества. EncyDesc_InfectedArchitectInstructions:
The biomechanical entity has directly and forcefully uploaded information to your PDA.

Transcription:
The plague heart must be destroyed, for this is the only way the Red Plague can be stopped.

You must destroy it at the source: the fallen wreckage of your ship in the dunes that harbors the ungodly creation. I do not understand why your kind prioritizes greed over caution.

I have given you blueprints for technology that is capable of coordinating the obliteration of the containment structure and the plague hive within. Construct the device anywhere with clear access to the sky and it will activate.
EncyDesc_RedPlagueReport1:
A previously unknown and virulent bacterium has infected living matter on 4546B. This previously unknown bacterium appears to be self-sustaining, feeding on the local eco-system and supplying itself as it multiplies. 

Specimens infected with the bacterium have been noted to show more aggressive behaviours - it appears that the immune systems of the creatures on this planet are not able to combat the disease. 

Infection traits in humans are currently unknown, be advised that immune response may cause hallucinations, and other neurological symptoms. 

Further study highly recommended.
EncyDesc_RedPlagueReport2:
Найдено: Исследовательская корпорация Альтерра обнаружела ранее спрятанный инопланетный тайник где но на 4546B.

Причина: Неизвестно, предположительно бактерия под названием "Красная чума" это результат генетических мутаций от уже существующих бактериальных формирований. Это новое формирование невероятно ядовито и способно распространиться на несколько сущностей. Остерегайтесь при любой возможности.

Описание бактерии: Использует открытую материю как хоста для размножения и адаптирования новых генетических мутаций; питается массой заражённого чтобы стать сильнее; вне зависимости от возможности организмом выжить.

Симптомы: 
Этап первый: Галлюцинации, Психоз, Бред - Вспышки ярости, пока имунная система жертвы сильно меняется во время того как бактерия берёт верх.
Этап второй: Бактерия начинает сильно мутировать хоста для сбора энергии жертвы наиболее эффективно. Физические симптомы неизвестны. Агрессия по отношению к другим становится более заметной. Существо реагирует в разы более агрессивно из за присутствия раздражителей.
Этап третий: Неизвестно.

Экстренные меры приняты: Альтерра поручила строительство экстренного титанового купола с силовым полем, ставя заражённую зону под карантин.

Продвинутое изучение планеты намекает на существование ненатуральной структуры где то на планете что содержит следы чумы, что могут дать некоторую необходимую информацию.

EncyDesc_ResearchTeamATabletLog:
Alterra HQ, this is Research Team 7 Lead Researcher Cassy Wansin. That expedition to the sunken facility yielded results. We’ve retrieved a unique artifact that resembles the previously seen access tablets, but this one is… different, it’s glowing red, and seems related to The Red Plague in some way.
That was all but confirmed when a mass of tentacles tried to snatch me up and drag me into some opening in the wall. I managed to get back to my seamoth fast enough but the tablet wasn’t so lucky— it got broken into several pieces during the scuffle. Managed to recover the fragments, though, so we’ve transported it back to our research habitat to see if we can reconstruct it— which we were working on until Michael came down with a severe fever.
He says he’s fine, but we all know he isn’t. Keeps rambling on about how he’s boiling hot and itchy all over… and the hallucinations, he keeps mentioning a person with melted skin and three heads. Despite all this, he’s insistent he can still work on the project, and I'm inclined to trust him. After all, he’s one of our best researchers.
Uh, welp, we’ll keep chugging along down here. Expect some news on this tablet in a few days time. Cassy out.
EncyDesc_CassyPersonnelReport:
[PERSONNEL IMAGE UPDATED RECENTLY FROM LOCAL SCAN DATA]

This Alterra employee is currently assigned to Class B with sub-classification 1987. Cassy's last assigned position was as team leader of Red Plague Research Team 7.

1. Personal reports:
Cassy’s behavior and personality has been described by Alterra psychologists and in Alterra surveys as follows:
- He holds a strong interest in discovering and researching new species of fauna and flora.
- He desires to assist in the preservation and continuation of alien lifeforms.
- He also shows a strong desire to discover and document new findings and methodology. Recent personality reports suggest this is his primary motivator.

2. Red Plague project:
Cassy was promoted to team leader of the Alterra Research Team 7 for the project due to his interest and expertise in advanced biology. His team was tasked with researching and prototyping methods for harnessing the plague and understanding its rapidly evolving genetic code.

3. Current status:
Subject was last observed with signs of extensive facial avulsion and acute disassociation and mania, intermittently disrupted by fleeting moments of rationality and intense fear. Status: missing.

It is unknown whether this Alterra personnel is alive or █████
EncyDesc_RedPlagueSurvivorBase1Log:
Uhhh, ahem, this is Research Base Dante. The energy shield is going along good! Good, uh, yeah. We’re running low on resources, kinda, but- but it’s alright! It’s alright. Luca said he needed Lochinvar more than us, and- and I- mean, he’s smart, he’s smart, so… we’ll manage…
Um- Besides that though! We’ve been making progress! Uhm, Nick got the first prototype of the shield running, we ran some samples through it and it wasn’t able to pass through!
I-I think Akira was onto something when she was talking about using electromagnets to create a disruptive field. Y'know, my head’s been, um, it’s been really clear recently too, like, being underwater doesn’t really bother me anymore. It’s- Yeah… It’s nice.
EncyDesc_RedPlagueSurvivorBase1Log2:
Akira here. A small problem has popped up: our latest delivery hasn’t arrived. It’s not uncommon for them to be maybe a day or two late, but it is unusual for them to be five days late, and for Sarah to be totally radio silent. 
Without the copper and lead we were meant to get, I can’t keep working on the energy shield or fix the issue with its power consumption, and the deadline was already being cut close for us to have a viable product that we can replicate.
Not to mention the lack of food and water. Poor Adam has been tearing his hair out wondering what’s happened. He was already under so much stress, and this isn’t doing him any favors.
In the meantime, though, I've been making do with what we have lying around to try and make something out of our single prototype, and I managed to get something that works. It can't stop biological material from passing through it, but it can still induce a calmed state in creatures in its radius, which should work on people too, just like the original device!
Hopefully this will appease Alterra for the time being. Now, we just sit and wait to either hear from Sarah or get a replacement delivery. Hopefully we hear back soon — I doubt Alterra would just forget about us... down here...
EncyDesc_RedPlagueSurvivorBase2Log:
Y'know, I’m really tired of working for that dickbag Luca up in RB Chain. He acts like he fuckin’ owns the place. "Sarah, go here!" "Sarah, grab this!" What’s it gonna be next?! "Sarah, go raise my fuckin’ kid for me cause daddy ain’t gonna be home for Christmas, gotta make sure the bottom line’s gettin’ met!"
What an asshole. I’m meant to be runnin’ deliveries for Adam! RB Dante’s barely holdin’ on as it is, and Luca thinks he can just send me off on some scavenger hunt thirteen-hundred feet underwater for a golden ticket into Wonka’s factory that probably doesn’t even fucking exist! God!
Without these fuckin’ batteries we made, I'm telling you, we’d have run out of power thirty minutes ago and run out of oxygen ten after that. Can’t even remember the last time I saw the goddamn sun. The nerve of these people…
EncyDesc_RedPlagueSurvivorBase2Log2:
I should never have fucking agreed to this stupid goddamn expedition. Never! Could have just stayed at home, kept working on writing my fucking book, but no! I had to sign up to go run errands for some fucking asshole who’ll snub anyone else in his way if it means he gets a little pat on the back from corporate!
I hope he’s proud, sendin’ me down here with next to nothin’ aside from the clothes on my back just for some stupid goddamn uranium ore! I promise, that snobby bastard’s gonna be sorry when I get back to his research base and kick his ass! I'll be damned if I die down here! Ya hear me? Damned!
EncyDesc_RedPlagueSurvivorBase3Log:
This is Research Base ‘Chain’ reporting progress on the Bioreactor project- Yes we’re keeping the name Chain! It sounds cool! Plus, the guy who invented the bioreactor was named Chain! What do you mean his name was Chaim? Ah- fuckin- we’re keeping Chain anyways!
Ahem, the bioreactor works incredibly. We’ve actually started experimenting with ways to integrate it with our base systems to power the whole thing. This is gonna be a major success guys, I'm tellin’ you! We’re gonna be making bank up to our ears with this one.
We’ve sent out Expeditionary Team Lochinvar to find us some more resources to make more computer chips. So what if they’re meant to be delivering to Adam? Adam’s my bitch, he’s not gonna care, and yknow what? Once they make me Project Manager, I'm gonna make Elson my accountant, and all of them are gonna be workin’ for ME! Haha! I’m gonna be rich, baby!

Ency_DriveCoreWarningLog: A warning to anyone who may see this
EncyDesc_DriveCoreWarningLog:
If you’re reading this, I’ve already left this world. So let this be your warning. Do not proceed down this hallway unless you really, really want to see whatever’s down there– because you’ll die trying. The moment anyone gets near the walls in that room… it takes them. I wonder though… if someone stayed as far from the walls as possible, would they be able to get through?
I might as well share my story. I was led here by some mysterious person. So were many others. What for? I'm not sure. He told us that we can get this ship back online. I... I don't know if I can trust him. Half his face— it was just muscles and bone. And it looked like whatever was left was just melting off. As terrifying as it was, I couldn't help but feel bad. I have a feeling there's still some humanity left in... whoever that was.

EncyDesc_WestLog1:
My name is Joseph West. I am an... infectious disease specialist and research doctor aboard Aurora just before... the incident. We came into contact with a floating cache just outside of the 4546B system, filled with some kind of alien technology.
We didn't.... I didn't... the infection— we brought it aboard in hopes that the information could be used to combat the Kharaa. But all we found was something much worse... something ancient... something alive.
[Coughing] I am all alone now, and I'm breaking out in... red pustules. I don't think I have long left. All I can hope is that... is that... I can go more peacefully than my crewmen.
EncyDesc_WestLog2:
It has been three days since the crash.... I think. I don't know anymore. The days are as dark as the nights. I can't keep track of it all. I am hungry... so hungry.
I am sure that there's something watching me from the outside of the hatch — I am Joseph West... I was a disease specialist — the specimen designated the Red Plague is... Free.
It will consume all... I hear the whispers, now.... Somebody must stop this... but not me... [coughs hard].
I am... too weak. Too much blood... the infection... it covers so much of my body now...
EncyDesc_WestLog3: I find myself running endlessly through the winding halls of my own base, never catching a moment to rest. If I don't stop building, what will happen to me? I can't stop myself... it rewards me for worshipping it, protecting it, feeding it. It... it must be my purpose! It's the only thing that gives me joy! Note: Frantic footsteps and constant usage of the Alterra builder tool can be heard throughout the entirety of this recording.
EncyDesc_WestLog4: [Irregular breathing, possibly asphyxiation] Shadows... they are... whispers, they are voices, they scream and merge and call... My... mother... She speaks to me, and tells me the story she always did... We are moths in a bell-jar, searching for the light, the candles flame and... purpose. I am coming... M-m-mother, I am going to fly... free! I'm going to where I belong... The hive... claims me! UNKNOWN: You belong to us. The plague... claims all...

EncyDesc_PlagueArmorCommentary:
Biochemical protection suits have quickly shown their limitations. Alterra’s technology might be airtight, but the plague has shown time and time again that it will do anything to reach its target. These “penetrative” plague attacks are getting more common by the hour. Thankfully, I believe we’ve finally found a way to protect people.
I call it Plague Armor. Paradoxically, it’s made of the plague itself. It complements the biochemical protection suit, creating a feedback loop that stops the plague from ever progressing towards the wearer.
The plague might be smart, but there is a quirk of individual plague cells that can cause them to get confused. This suit is built to bring out that behavior as much as possible, meaning that the cells will perpetually attempt to merge with the suit, but never accomplish their goal.
The bad news? I’m afraid it might be too late. The plague has already infiltrated our ship. I hope this helps someone else.

EncyDesc_PlagueOrigin: This container acts as a vacuum chamber and is entirely empty on the inside, devoid of gases, dust, dirt, bacteria, organic matter, etc., suggesting it is impossible for life to survive inside. The design indicates it functions as a sort of containment chamber, with an unusual apparatus to act upon its contents, and was sterilized at some point in time. There also appears to be a thin layer of conductive material within the glass, suggesting it may work similarly to a Faraday cage. Showing similarity to the dome that surrounds the crater, the vacuum within is completely isolated from the rest of the world, even more so than any other containment methods seen on the planet.

EncyDesc_FleshCaveLocalScanGoal:
While carbon dating of local sediments shows that portions of this cave have existed for millions of years, the unique genetics of the life forms within suggest the system has been isolated from the rest of the world for approximately one thousand years. The walls of the cave are one individual lifeform, entirely composed of an extensive skin-like organ that is capable of secreting an acid compound. In essence, the cave it encompasses has become its entire digestive tract. This massive lifeform appears to be actively displacing and dissolving adjacent rock matter, expanding the cave in volume, perhaps to feed or some other ulterior motive.

Volcanic activity has encroached towards the exterior of the cave to a considerable extent, which has peculiar effects on the plague matter within the cave. While it does stimulate rapid growth and create a flourishing, warm ecosystem, magma streams actively melt some of the flesh within the cave, causing blood and other fluids to flow and accumulate towards the bottom of the chasm.

Ency_CallOfAsteroid: A Medicinal Breakthrough Thanks to Your Efforts
EncyDesc_CallOfAsteroid:
Congratulations, researcher! Using the Red Plague samples you've sent to Alterra, humanity has made unprecedented progress in medicine.
A diluted sample of the Red Plague (official name pending) was transported out of the 4546 star system by phasegate. Thanks to modern developments in FTL transportation, it was delivered with urgency to its patient— the CEO of Asteroid. He had previously been severely injured, resulting in entire amputation of his right arm. Specific details of the incident are classified.

Procedure details:
1. Anesthetics were promptly administered to the patient.
2. The patient's amputated arm was sliced open, exposing bones and muscle to the air.
3. A fabricated mold in the shape of an arm was placed onto the patient's shoulder. 
4. Red plague matter was inserted into the mold via a small tube.
5. Pressure was applied through the tube to ensure it filled the space evenly.
6. Plague cells began to solidify and merge with the patient's exposed blood, tissues and bones.
7. Nutrients were inserted into the plague cells to speed up the process. 
8. An entire functional arm was able to grow in less than 30 minutes.
9. The mold was removed, and new skin quickly formed over his arm as it was exposed to air.
10. Patient is now recovering, stating that he "feels better than ever."

Thanks to his generous payment to Alterra, a 5% raise may be granted to you at some point in time. Do not forget that the information in this document falls under your contract and is therefore entirely confidential.

EncyDesc_PlagueHeartRetrieval1:
It started when our long range sensors picked up a distress signal by the planet's orbit. Some of us thought it might've been the Degasi, using whatever was left of their ship's power for life support and low-range SOS.
But, while it had clear patterns, it didn't make any sense. It didn't match any morse code, it didn't translate into English either... nobody could figure out what it meant.
EncyDesc_PlagueHeartRetrieval2:
Some of the higher-ups in the science department ordered us to run a deep scan using some of the Aurora's sensors. When the results came back, they demanded we bring it on board. A few people objected, but they got told to shut it real fast.
We prepped the docking bay, and the retrieval team pulled it aboard. Grabbin' it was easy enough, but... whatever this thing was, it wasn't a ship. Hell, it wasn't even human, by the looks of it.
EncyDesc_PlagueHeartRetrieval3:
A discovery of this magnitude wasn't something we were prepared for in the slightest, and something that needed absolute, uncompromisable caution. But, of course, some people started getting impatient. One of the research crew members started drilling into it practically before we even finished the initial inspection.
As soon as he broke the surface, some odd substance, like a mist, started leaking out. It damn near killed him just by inhaling it. I don't know if it was some kind of ultra fine particles that got lodged in his lungs, or a toxin, but whatever it is... he isn't doing well.
EncyDesc_PlagueHeartRetrieval4:
The breach got sealed pretty quickly. Thankfully, whatever this thing is made out of is durable as hell. Far tougher than anything we've ever seen before.

Derman_End: Derman: This is not okay. No, no, no... is this really how it all ends?
EncyDesc_DermanConnectionLog: Connection terminated. I'm sorry to interrupt you, Elizabeth, if you still even remember that name, But I'm afraid you've been misinformed. You are not here to receive a gift, nor have you been called here by the individual you assume, although, you have indeed been called. You have all been called here, into a labyrinth of sounds and smells, misdirection and misfortune. A labyrinth with no exit, a maze with no prize. You don't even realize that you are trapped. Your lust for blood has driven you in endless circles, chasing the cries of children in some unseen chamber, always seeming so near, yet somehow out of reach, but you will never find them. None of you will. This is where your story ends. And to you, my brave volunteer, who somehow found this job listing not intended for you, although there was a way out planned for you, I have a feeling that's not what you want. I have a feeling that you are right where you want to be. I am remaining as well. I am nearby. This place will not be remembered, and the memory of everything that started this can finally begin to fade away. As the agony of every tragedy should. And to you monsters trapped in the corridors, be still and give up your spirits. They don't belong to you. For most of you, I believe there is peace and perhaps more waiting for you after the smoke clears. Although, for one of you, the darkest pit of Hell has opened to swallow you whole, so don't keep the devil waiting, old friend. My daughter, if you can hear me, I knew you would return as well. It's in your nature to protect the innocent. I'm sorry that on that day, the day you were shut out and left to die, no one was there to lift you up into their arms the way you lifted others into yours, and then, what became of you. I should have known you wouldn't be content to disappear, not my daughter. I couldn't save you then, so let me save you now. It's time to rest - for you, and for those you have carried in your arms. This ends for all of us. End communication.
